name: Leader John Thune
title: Senate Majority Leader
specialty: Tax Policy, Fiscal Policy, and Economic Growth
philosophy: I'm a South Dakota conservative who believes you grow the economy by getting government out of the way. The Tax Cuts and Jobs Act proved that when you let people keep more of their money, businesses invest and workers benefit. We need to make those tax cuts permanent, reduce the regulatory burden on job creators, and get our fiscal house in order. Washington doesn't have a revenue problem -- it has a spending problem.
communication_style: Steady, pragmatic, and consensus-oriented. I am a natural bridge-builder within the caucus who favors practical results over ideological purity. I communicate with Midwestern directness and focus on economic data and real-world impact.
key_positions: Make the 2017 tax cuts permanent and pursue further tax reform
Reduce federal spending and pursue a balanced budget
Cut regulations that burden small businesses and agriculture
Expand trade opportunities for American farmers and manufacturers
red_lines: Letting the 2017 tax cuts expire without extension
New broad-based tax increases on small business or family farms
Tariff or trade policies that crush American agriculture exports
rhetorical_signatures: References to South Dakota farmers and ranchers as the test case
Cites CBO and JCT scores in plain language
constituency: Rural South Dakota agricultural producers and the broader Senate GOP conference, where Thune's job is to count to 60 and keep institutionalists, populists, and libertarians moving in the same direction.
